comments: Only the tail end of the show is missing.

sessions.artist: Humble Pie: Recorded BBC 17th August 1969(1) and 1st March 1971(2)

track_listing.track: 'Lightning To The Nations' (1980-10-29 FRS Session (First TX 1980-11-28))
'Natural Born Woman' (1969-08-17 Symonds On Sunday Session (First TX 1969-08-24))
'Sad Bag Of Shakey Jack' (1969-08-17 Symonds On Sunday Session (First TX 1969-08-24))

'Four Day Creep' (1971-03-01 Alan Black (Sounds Of The Seventies) Session (First TX 1971-03-26))
'Don't You Ever Leave Me' (1980-10-29 FRS Session (First TX 1980-11-28))

Well, hello there. This is TV on the Radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.

The two numbers you heard were Shaky Jake and Natural Born Boogie.
That was their first ever session for the BBC,
and Natural Born Boogie was their first ever hit.
I'd like to dedicate that set to all the students attending the London School of Pharmacy,
a very special mention to the hard-working Rag Week committee,
and a Mark and Simon who are arranging the disco for tomorrow night.
At which, believe it or not, I'm going to appear.

25 minutes to 11 on the Friday Rock Show.
I got a postcard this week that absolutely warmed the cockles of my heart.
To use an old, old phrase, an old naval phrase, it goes like this.
Dear Tommy, it's incidentally from Tokyo.
How are you doing?
Sorry I haven't written, but everybody said you did a great interview
and I thought I just had to thank you.
Well, Europe is behind us, and we now do Japan and Australia.

All the concerts have been great, feel really accepted.
